The day of the interview had been long and exhausting, and as the clock ticked closer to the appointed time, the nerves and anxiety that had been simmering within me were now threatening to boil over. I could feel the sweat drip down the side of my face and I was constantly on edge, anticipating the worst.
But as the door opened and a young man stepped out, I felt a wave of relief wash over me. He was tall, with a rugged good looks that immediately made me forget about my nerves. My hands shook as I walked into the conference room, the walls clamoring with the sound of chatter and buzzing machines. But within seconds, my nerves settled, and I took a deep breath, preparing to answer the questions with confidence and ease.
The interviewer asked me about my background and why I was excited to join the company. I thought for a moment, taking a deep breath and focusing on my answers.

The clock ticked on as I sat across from the interviewer, the room filling with anticipation.
